name: Crown Agents Investment Management
description: Acuity Ventures is a venture capital firm that invests in early to growth-stage technology companies in Africa. Acuity leverages its partners' strong investment track record in technology and deep data science expertise to capture and use information to inform investment decisions and help investee companies realize outsized growth. Acuity's data-driven strategy allows it to recognize and amplify product-market fit, providing a more accurate quantitative view to early stage-investing at a company stage when accounting metrics are a less reliable predictor of company success. Acuity focuses on enterprise, soft infrastructure, and platforms that integrate Africa.

[](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.cbinsights.com/company/aavishkaar-capital)[](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=https://www.cbinsights.com/company/aavishkaar-capital)[](https://mail.google.com/mail/?view=cm&fs=1&tf=1&to=&su=Check%20out%20Aavishkaar%20Capital's%20Profile%20on%20CB%20Insights&body=I%20thought%20you%E2%80%99d%20find%20Aavishkaar%20Capital's%20page%20interesting:%20https://www.cbinsights.com/company/aavishkaar-capital)

## Founded Year
2001
## Stage
Unattributed VC | Alive
## About Aavishkaar Capital
Aavishkaar Capital operates as a venture capital firm focusing on impact investing. The company provides financial support to entrepreneurs building sustainable enterprises, particularly those that can generate commercial returns while serving humanity sustainably. It primarily invests in businesses that cater to excluded communities and remote geographies, with a focus on scalable rural-focused enterprises. It was founded in 2001 and is based in Mumbai, India.
## Headquarters Location
Naman Center, C-31, G Block, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra East 2nd Floor
Mumbai, 400051,
India

## Latest Aavishkaar Capital News
[Aavishkaar Capital led $5 M funding raised by Poshs Metal](https://incubees.com/aavishkaar-capital-led-5-m-funding-raised-by-poshs-metal/)
Apr 24, 2025
The auto-grade flat steel processing company Poshs Metal Industries has raised Rs 43 crore ($5 million) in a funding round headed by Aavishkaar Capital. Poshs Metal will be able to better serve OEM clients by using the proceeds to support the construction of a new processing facility in Aurangabad. Poshs Metal, a steel service center for tier I automakers, was established by Ashok Kapoor. Tata Steel and it have had a 25-year exclusive partnership for the processing and distribution of steel in Western India. Volvo, Volkswagen, Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, and Tata Motors are important customers. Value-added services like scrap processing, in-house packaging, and steel blank customization are provided by the company. It replaced wooden pallets with an environmentally friendly steel pallet system. Aavishkaar’s $220 million global supply chain support fund, which assists SMEs in Asia and Africa that prioritize inclusive and sustainable practices, has made seven investments. The KfW Development Bank in Germany is supporting the fund.
Apr 22, 2025
[Aavishkaar Capital Invests INR 43 Crores in Poshs Metal Industries](https://www.autocarpro.in/NEWS/aavishkaar-capital-invests-inr-43-crores-in-poshs-metal-industries-126011)

### Aavishkaar Capital Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
* When was Aavishkaar Capital founded?
Aavishkaar Capital was founded in 2001.
* Where is Aavishkaar Capital's headquarters?
Aavishkaar Capital's headquarters is located at Naman Center, C-31, G Block, Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra East, Mumbai.
* What is Aavishkaar Capital's latest funding round?
Aavishkaar Capital's latest funding round is Unattributed VC.
* Who are the investors of Aavishkaar Capital?
Investors of Aavishkaar Capital include Blue7, Jayesh Parekh, AppWorks Ventures, Artha Impact and Blue Earth Capital.
* Who are Aavishkaar Capital's competitors?
Competitors of Aavishkaar Capital include Red Fort Capital and 5 more.

## Compare Aavishkaar Capital to Competitors
I
[Incofin Investment Management](https://www.cbinsights.com/investor/incofin-investment-management)
Incofin Investment Management (Incofin IM) manages funds that invest in microfinance institutions (MFIs) in developing countries. It provides financial services, such as credits, savings, and insurance, to people who cannot have recourse to regular banks. It focuses on rural microfinancing and aims to reach out to people who live in more secluded rural areas and who are active in the agricultural sector. It was founded in 2001 and is based in Antwerp, Belgium.
![SV Creditline Logo](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/cbi-image-service-prd/original/dffee4d5-09b6-44c8-8136-bbf20b20aefa.png)
[SV Creditline](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/svcl)
SV Creditline operates in the financial services sector, providing microcredit services through a joint liability group model. It offers collateral-free loans aimed at supporting income generation activities, primarily serving the rural population. It was founded in 2011 and is based in Gurugram, India.
![Jagaran Microfin Logo](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/cbi-image-service-prd/original/2790d086-9b6a-4c2f-ba08-aa301f0ce3ba.png)
[Jagaran Microfin](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/jagaran-microfin)
Jagaran Microfin is a microfinance institution providing financial services to economically challenged sections of society in rural areas. The company offers various financial products aimed at individuals and small businesses, with an emphasis on women entrepreneurs. Jagaran Microfin serves sectors needing financial inclusion and support for business development, aiming to reduce social inequalities and enhance community livelihoods. It was founded in 2010 and is based in Kolkata, India.
N
[Northern Arc Investments](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/northern-arc-investments)
Northern Arc Investments is a financial services company focused on financial inclusion and impact investing in India. The company invests in entities that provide financial services to economically underserved individuals and businesses, aiming to improve their access to capital and financial products. Northern Arc Investments primarily serves sectors that promote social and economic development, such as education, gender equality, and sustainable economic growth. It was founded in 1989 and is based in Chennai, India.
![Saija Finance Logo](https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/cbi-image-service-prd/original/ffc75074-f025-4927-a2db-46dea183b215.jpeg)
[Saija Finance](https://www.cbinsights.com/company/saija-finance)
Saija Finance is a non-banking financial company specializing in microfinance services. The company provides financial services to urban and rural poor, as well as micro and small businessmen, focusing on underserved regions. Saija Finance primarily serves the microfinance sector. It was founded in 2007 and is based in India.
R
[Rang De](https://www.cbinsights.com/investor/rang-de)
Rang De operates a peer-to-peer lending platform championing microcredit access for rural India. The company enables individuals to invest in first-time borrowers, with a focus on farmers, entrepreneurs, and students from low-income households. It was founded in 2008 and is based in Bangalore, India.
## Discover the right solution for your team
The CB Insights tech market intelligence platform analyzes millions of data points on vendors, products, partnerships, and patents to help your team find their next technology solution.

contact.address: Off Eastern Express Highway, Sewri-Chembur Road 01, AWFIS, Space Solutions, 4th Floor, VIOS Towers

founders.name: Lexi Novitske
founders.bio: Founded in 2018 by Lexi Novitske after acquiring the Singularity Investments portfolio; focuses on technology startups in Africa, investing in Seed and Series A rounds.

founders.name: Ike Eze
founders.bio: Managing Partner at Beta.Ventures and a three-time Silicon Valley founder with a successful $130 million exit. He has founded and exited three Silicon Valley-based companies, including QSpace, which was acquired by Experian for $130 million. He’s held roles at global firms like AT&T, Bechtel, Boston Consulting Group, and eTranzact, where he served as an executive director. Eze also serves on multiple startup boards across financial services, logistics, and technology.
